verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is annotated as nuclear-resident (NCBI/Alliance of Genome Resources). Unlike IZUMO1, which is a sperm-surface protein critical for egg-sperm fusion, IZUMO4 lacks the features of its surface-displaying paralogs. It has no TM domain, no GPI signal, and no documented outer-leaflet localization. The IZUMO family includes members with surface roles, but IZUMO4 is the divergent nuclear member. No cell-surface therapeutic programs, surface proteomics hits, or contextual surface-display evidence are known for this protein.
